verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: God will not have us listen to the words and sentences contained in the Scriptures carelessly, but with much attention.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This is why the blessed David hath prefixed in many places to his Psalms the title “for understanding,” and hath said, “Open Thou mine eyes, that I may behold wondrous things out of Thy Law.” ( Ps. xxxii.

verses.text: And after him his son again shows that we ought to “seek out wisdom as silver, and to make merchandise of her rather than of gold.” ( Prov. ii.

verses.text: 14 [partially quoted]; John v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And the Lord when He exhorts the Jews to “search the Scriptures,” the more urges us to the enquiry, for He would not thus have spoken if it were possible to comprehend them immediately at the first reading.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: No one would ever search for what is obvious and at hand, but for that which is wrapt in shadow, and which must be found after much enquiry; and so to arouse us to the search He calls them “hidden treasure.” ( Prov. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 4; Matt. xiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: These words are said to us that we may not apply ourselves to the words of the Scriptures carelessly or in a chance way, but with great exactness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if any one listen to what is said in them without enquiring into the meaning, and receive all so as it is spoken, according to the letter, he will suppose many unseemly things of God, will admit of Him that He is a man, that He is made of brass, is wrathful, is furious, and many opinions yet worse than these.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if he fully learn the sense that lies beneath, he will be freed from all this unseemliness. ( Rev. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The very text which now lies before us says, that God has a bosom, a thing proper to bodily substances, yet no one is so insane as to imagine, that He who is without body is a body.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In order then that we may properly interpret the entire passage according to its spiritual meaning, let us search it through from its beginning.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “No man hath seen God at any time.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By what connection of thought does the Apostle come to say this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: After showing the exceeding greatness of the gifts of Christ, and the infinite difference between them and those ministered by Moses, he would add the reasonable cause of the difference.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Moses, as being a servant, was minister of lower things, but Christ being Lord and King, and the King’s Son, brought to us things far greater, being ever with the Father, and beholding Him continually; wherefore He saith, “No man hath seen God at any time.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then shall we answer to the most mighty of voice, Esaias, when he says, “I saw the Lord sitting upon a throne high and lifted up” ( Isa. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 1 ); and to John himself testifying of Him, that “he said these things when he had seen His glory”? ( c. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What also to Ezekiel? for he too beheld Him sitting above the Cherubim. ( Ezek. i. and x.)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What to Daniel? for he too saith, “The Ancient of days did sit” ( Dan. vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What to Moses himself, saying, “Show me Thy Glory, that I may see Thee so as to know Thee.” ( Ex. xxxiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 13 , partly from LXX.)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And Jacob took his name from this very thing, being called “Israel”; for Israel is “one that sees God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And others have seen him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then saith John, “No man hath seen God at any time”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is to declare, that all these were instances of (His) condescension, not the vision of the Essence itself unveiled.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For had they seen the very Nature, they would not have beheld It under different forms, since that is simple, without form, or parts, or bounding lines.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It sits not, nor stands, nor walks: these things belong all to bodies.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But how He Is, He only knoweth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this He hath declared by a certain prophet, saying, “I have multiplied visions, and used similitudes by the hands of the prophets” ( Hos. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 10 ), that is, “I have condescended, I have not appeared as I really was.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since His Son was about to appear in very flesh, He prepared them from old time to behold the substance of God, as far as it was possible for them to see It; but what God really is, not only have not the prophets seen, but not even angels nor archangels.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If you ask them, you shall not hear them answering anything concerning His Essence, but sending up, “Glory to God in the Highest, on earth peace, good will towards men.” ( Luke ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If you desire to learn something from Cherubim or Seraphim, you shall hear the mystic song of His Holiness, and that “heaven and earth are full of His glory.” ( Isa. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If you enquire of the higher powers, you shall but find that their one work is the praise of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Praise ye Him,” saith David, “all His hosts.” ( Ps. cxlviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But the Son only Beholds Him, and the Holy Ghost.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How can any created nature even see the Uncreated?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If we are absolutely unable clearly to discern any incorporeal power whatsoever, even though created, as has been often proved in the case of angels, much less can we discern the Essence which is incorporeal and uncreated.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore Paul saith, “Whom no man hath seen, nor can see.” ( 1 Tim. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Does then this special attribute belong to the Father only, not to the Son?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Away with the thought.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It belongs also to the Son; and to show that it does so, hear Paul declaring this point, and saying, that He “is the Image of the invisible God.” ( Col. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now if He be the Image of the Invisible, He must be invisible Himself, for otherwise He would not be an “image.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And wonder not that Paul saith in another place, “God was manifested in the Flesh” ( 1 Tim. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 16 ); because the manifestation took place by means of the flesh, not according to (His) Essence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Besides, Paul shows that He is invisible, not only to men, but also to the powers above, for after saying, “was manifested in the Flesh,” he adds, “was seen of angels.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So that even to angels He then became visible, when He put on the Flesh; but before that time they did not so behold Him, because even to them His Essence was invisible.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “How then,” asks some one, “did Christ say, ‘Despise not one of these little ones, for I tell you, that their angels do always behold the face of My Father which is in heaven’? ( Matt. xviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hath then God a face, and is He bounded by the heavens?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Who so mad as to assert this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then is the meaning of the words?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As when He saith, “Blessed are the pure in heart, for they shall see God” ( Matt. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 8 ), He means that intellectual vision which is possible to us, and the having God in the thoughts; so in the case of angels, we must understand that by reason of their pure and sleepless nature they do nothing else, but always image to themselves God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And therefore Christ saith, that “No man knoweth the Father, save the Son.” ( Matt. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then, are we all in ignorance?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: God forbid; but none knoweth Him as the Son knoweth Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As then many have seen Him in the mode of vision permitted to them, but no one has beheld His Essence, so many of us know God, but what His substance can be none knoweth, save only He that was begotten of Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For by “knowledge” He here means an exact idea and comprehension, such as the Father hath of the Son.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “As the Father knoweth Me, even so know I the Father.” ( c. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe, therefore, with what fullness the Evangelist speaks; for having said that “no man hath seen God at any time,” he does not go on to say, “that the Son who hath seen, hath declared Him,” but adds something beyond “seeing” by the words, “Who is in the bosom of the Father”; because, “to dwell in the bosom” is far more than “to see.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he that merely “seeth” hath not an in every way exact knowledge of the object, but he that “dwelleth in the bosom” can be ignorant of nothing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now lest when thou hearest that “none knoweth the Father, save the Son,” thou shouldest assert that although He knoweth the Father more than all, yet He knoweth not how great He is, the Evangelist says that He dwells in the bosom of the Father; and Christ Himself declares, that He knoweth Him as much as the Father knoweth the Son.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Ask therefore the gainsayer, “Tell me, doth the Father know the Son?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if he be not mad, he will certainly answer “Yes.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then ask again; “Doth He see and know Him with exact vision and knowledge?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Doth He know clearly what He Is?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He will certainly confess this also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: From this next collect the exact comprehension the Son has of the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He saith, “As the Father knoweth me, even so know I the Father” ( c. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 15 ); and in another place, “Not that any man hath seen the Father, save He which is of God.” ( c. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore, as I said, the Evangelist mentions “the bosom,” to show all this to us by that one word; that great is the affinity and nearness of the Essence, that the knowledge is nowise different, that the power is equal.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the Father would not have in His bosom one of another essence, nor would He have dared, had He been one amongst many servants, to live in the bosom of his Lord, for this belongs only to a true Son, to one who has much confidence towards His Father, and who is in nothing inferior to Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wouldest thou learn also His eternity?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hear what Moses saith concerning the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When he asked what he was commanded to answer should the Jews enquire of him, “Who it was that had sent him,” he heard these words: “Say, I AM hath sent me.” ( Ex. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now the expression “I AM,” is significative of Being ever, and Being without beginning, of Being really and absolutely.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this also the expression, “Was in the beginning,” declares, being indicative of Being ever; so that John uses this word to show that the Son Is from everlasting to everlasting in the bosom of the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For that you may not from the sameness of name, suppose that He is some one of those who are made sons by grace, first, the article is added, distinguishing Him from those by grace.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if this does not content you, if you still look earthwards, hear a name more absolute than this, “Only-Begotten.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If even after this you still look below, “I will not refuse,” says he, (St. John,) “to apply to God a term belonging to man, I mean the word ‘bosom,’ only suspect nothing degrading.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Dost thou see the lovingkindness and carefulness of the Lord?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: God applies to Himself unworthy expressions, that even so thou mayest see through them, and have some great and lofty thought of Him; and dost thou tarry below?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For tell me, wherefore is that gross and carnal word “bosom” employed in this place?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Is it that we may suppose God to be a body?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Away, he by no means saith so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why then is it spoken? for if by it neither the genuineness of the Son is established, nor that God is not a body, the word, because it serves no purpose, is superfluously thrown in.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why then is it spoken?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For I shall not desist from asking thee this question.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Is it not very plain, that it is for no other reason but that by it we might understand the genuineness of the Only-Begotten, and His Co-eternity with the Father?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He hath declared Him,” saith John.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What hath he declared?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That “no man hath seen God at any time”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That “God is one”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But this all the other prophets testify, and Moses continually exclaims, “The Lord thy God is one Lord” ( Deut. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 4 ); and Esaias, “Before Me there was no God formed, neither shall there be after me.” ( Isa. xliii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What more then have we learned from “the Son which is in the bosom of the Father”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What from “the Only-Begotten”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In the first place, these very words were uttered by His working; in the next place, we have received a teaching that is far clearer, and learned that “God is a spirit, and they that worship Him must worship Him in spirit and in truth” ( c. iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 24 ); and again, that it is impossible to see God; “that no man knoweth” Him, “save the Son” ( Matt. xi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 27 ); that He is the Father of the true and Only-Begotten; and all other things that are told us of Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But the word “hath declared” shows the plainer and clearer teaching which He gave not to the Jews only but to all the world, and established.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To the prophets not even all the Jews gave heed, but to the Only-Begotten Son of God all the world yielded and obeyed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So the “declaration” in this place shows the greater clearness of His teaching, and therefore also He is called “Word,” and “Angel of great Counsel.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since then we have been vouchsafed a larger and more perfect teaching, God having no longer spoken by the prophets, but “having in these last days spoken to us by His Son” ( Heb. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 1 ), let us show forth a conversation far higher than theirs, and suitable to the honor bestowed on us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Strange would it be that He should have so far lowered Himself, as to choose to speak to us no longer by His servants, but by His own mouth, and yet we should show forth nothing more than those of old.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They had Moses for their teacher, we, Moses’ Lord.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then exhibit a heavenly wisdom worthy of this honor, and let us have nothing to do with earth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It was for this that He brought His teaching from heaven above, that He might remove our thoughts thither, that we might be imitators of our Teacher according to our power.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But how may we become imitators of Christ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By acting in everything for the common good, and not merely seeking our own.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For even Christ,” saith Paul, “pleased not Himself, but as it is written, The reproaches of them that reproached Thee fell on Me.” ( Rom. xv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 3; Ps. lxix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let no one therefore seek his own.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In truth, a man (really) seeks his own good when he looks to that of his neighbor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What is their good is ours; we are one body, and parts and limbs one of another.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us not then be as though we were rent asunder.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let no one say, “such a person is no friend of mine, nor relation, nor neighbor, I have nought to do with him, how shall I approach, how address him?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Though he be neither relation nor friend, yet he is a man, who shares the same nature with thee, owns the same Lord, is thy fellow-servant, and fellow-sojourner, for he is born in the same world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if besides he partakes of the same faith, behold he hath also become a member of thee: for what friendship could work such union, as the relationship of faith?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And our intimacy one with another must not be such nearness only as friends ought to show to friends, but such as is between limb and limb, because no man can possibly discover any intimacy greater than this sort of friendship and fellowship.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As then you cannot say, “Whence arises my intimacy and connection with this limb?” (that would be ridiculous;) so neither can you say so in the case of your brother.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “We are all baptized into one body” ( 1 Cor. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 13 ), saith Paul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Wherefore into one body?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That we be not rent asunder, but preserve the just proportions of that one body by our intercourse and friendship one with another.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us not then despise one another, lest we be neglectful of ourselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For no man ever yet hated his own flesh, but nourisheth and cherisheth it.” ( Eph. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And therefore God hath given to us but one habitation, this earth, hath distributed all things equally, hath lighted one sun for us all, hath spread above us one roof, the sky, made one table, the earth, bear food for us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And another table hath He given far better than this, yet that too is one, (those who share our mysteries understand my words,) one manner of birth He hath bestowed on all, the spiritual, we all have one country, that in the heavens, of the same cup drink we all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He hath not bestowed on the rich man a gift more abundant and more honorable, and on the poor one more mean and small, but He hath called all alike.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He hath given carnal things with equal regard to all, and spiritual in like manner.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Whence then proceeds the great inequality of conditions in life?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: From the avarice and pride of the wealthy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But let not, brethren, let not this any longer be; and when matters of universal interest and more pressing necessity bring us together, let us not be divided by things earthly and insignificant: I mean, by wealth and poverty, by bodily relationship, by enmity and friendship; for all these things are a shadow, nay less substantial than a shadow, to those who possess the bond of charity from above.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then preserve this unbroken, and none of those evil spirits will be able to enter in, who cause division in so perfect union; to which may we all attain by the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, by whom and with whom, to the Father and the Holy Ghost, be glory, now and ever, and world without end.
